I'm like, I gotta figure this stuff out so I'm listening to these courses and and there's one like resounding theme I keep hearing throughout the whole thing with this with their with them their courses and if they kept saying over and over and over again It says look you as a as a teacher, right? Like you want to go up there and teach and give and share So the problem is you're doing that you're doing your audience a huge disservice because there's no way in 90 minutes You can change someone's life Like you can't give them the tools and the training and everything they need to be successful It said your only job your only responsibility during that 90 minutes is to inspire them and Have give them belief that what you are able to do will work for them. That's it Okay, so I'm gonna repeat that your job in that 90 minutes is to inspire them and to give them belief That what it is that you are selling can help them Okay, that's it If you can inspire them and if you can give them a belief that what you have will help them they will give you money That's it. Okay, you impressing them with how much you know how good of a teacher you are will not Well first off not gonna help them because they're gonna leave with an in with an incomplete You know understanding to be able to implement what you showed them and second off They're not gonna give you money. Okay, and so you have to stand over and over and over again I was like that seems so backwards from my from logic for me It should be like where we get the most value gets the most money, right? And that's true in parts of life, but not in a webinar or a stage pitch when you're selling it's different and so And so I said, you know what fine I'm gonna just trust these guys and so I started learning how they were doing their stuff I watched their presentations and I model they're doing I went to my next event Had my new presentation where I instead of teaching a whole bunch of stuff I was trying to inspire them and give them belief that what I had to sell would help could help them and in my first pitch and I Remember I sold six people the thousand bucks of pop six thousand dollars, which again was a lot of money I was just like boom like okay. I was at a Dan Kennedy event and Armand Moran the guy who ran the big seminar my very first seminar went to was speaking He gets up on stage and he's selling the thousand dollar thing and he literally closed over like I don't know it's like four or five hundred thousand dollars in sales Like it was the most amazing thing I'd ever seen and I was just watching it was like it was like watching someone do art right like their painting is the most amazing thing in the world and And I'm watching this and and afterwards like I'm kind of lingering around someone talk to him Just tell him, you know, like how cool he is and I was actually I was speaking at his event two weeks later first time I ever spoke in the big seminar which I was excited for because that was my first event I ever went to right and so He does this pitch and he makes all the money and then the end of it everyone's talking and they finally leave and He walks up to me. So first thing I do is the second I start my webinar I typically started about five to ten minutes early. I get on there and I'm not just quiet. Hey guys This is Russell. I'll be on a little bit and quiet I want energy and emotion and noise and things happening right when I get on okay It's the first thing I get on like hey, this is Russell Brunson you guys I am so excited for the webinar today if you are just logging in we're about to get started this webinar we'll be talking about blah blah blah whatever the thing is we're talking about saying it's gonna be amazing and In the word you started about six and a half minutes with six and a half minutes from right now We're gonna get started and I'm excited for you guys We had tons of you guys logging in right now and and for those of you guys are just jumping on I want to welcome you to the webinar My name is Russell Brunson. I'm gonna be your host today And we're about five minutes away from from go time and I'm just excited to have you guys here and when the webinar starts I'm gonna be covering one of my favorite topics in the world, which is blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah Okay, now for those of you guys who are just jumping on and welcome you to the webinar About four and a half minutes from you see I'm doing this So there's constant talking for ten minutes where I'm just talking in a loop because people are logging in every about thirty seconds Okay, well you'll see like if you have a big webinar like every second new people are logging in okay And so I'm on like a little twenty to thirty second track where I'm just looping this and looping this and looping I'm trying to say different ways so it's a little interesting and exciting But I'm telling them how much long you know what how much longer is what's the hook? We'll try to get on in a few more minutes It just changes the whole state right I'm very big and like When if you guys have ever studied NLP a lot talks a lot about pre frames and states and things like that like I can affect your state Really really easily based on a frame that I'm taking you through right so if I want you to buy if I'm gonna be asking you for money over here Everything else is happening before here is a frame right each each every time you move from one step that you're entering through a frame And so I want to control that frame the best I can and make that experience amazing because the better that is the more likely you're Gonna buy you're gonna do it on the next page and the next page and keep moving through right so it starts with me With a whole bunch of energy getting excited for what's about to happen Okay, all right now when the webinars about to start make hey guys, we are at the top of the hour We always start on time. That's it Now they know like at the end of webinar He's gonna show me his funnel and show me how to knock it off if I get those two things this hour has been has been successful Okay, does that make sense you guys so you've got to you've got to give him a bold promise Because that's what they're gonna judge you by so many times people jump in the web and I start teaching at the end of people Like why was I even like why was I even on that thing was so weird, right? So you got it. You've got to you got to do that All right, so bold promise second thing is we got a hook to the end So if you look at mine down here slide number six I say now I got I got a gift for you guys for everyone who stays till the end I created a sales funnel called funnology and I'm gonna give you this funnel for free In fact, this looks like right here. See those four pages that sells a product here I'm gonna give you guys this as a gift for free just for staying till the end Nothing else you have to pay anything you get this for free if you stay till the end Okay, what I do well I've got their attention so I only got it for a few minutes before a lot of me start jumping off I've got to grab them and and I've got to give them a hook to the end Why should you say to the end? He does have to be a free funnel sometimes like one of my webinars when I best selling ones was my high ticket secrets And I say how many you guys want to see my call center? Okay? So it can be it doesn't have to be something tangible it can help You know, I see a lot people go like at the end of something give away a free iPod Which I'm not a big fan of those because it's so unrelated now You get people staying just for you know for whatever but if you can find something that's just kind of interesting and unique that some Like you know, I'm I'm willing to give to sell to you because they're basically selling you their time I'm gonna sell you this time in the hopes that I'll get the thing at the end. Okay? All right, so here's kind of the concept first thing I'm a huge believer in this is your webinar has to focus on one thing. What is the one thing you're trying to help somebody to accomplish? Okay, I had a Conversation with Perry Belcher's one of my friends and he was talking about he's a great copywriter, but he was talking about how he How he had gone and they done bunch of tests on their different cells Videos and webinars and things they had done he said that he said that they found is that if they ever tried to convince somebody Of more than one thing in a sales video So two things or three things or whatever that was that it would nine hands out ten would bomb and the more simple the the concept was and There was only one thing Like the offers made the most money all one thing that wasn't common was they were all focused on one thing one thing You're trying to convince somebody up. Okay, and so for you your webinars not about you know I'm gonna show you that the seven different ways to increase traffic to your website like that's that's not it It's understanding like with the one thing. What's the one thing? that That they've got to understand if they believe that one thing then then Everything else fits in the everything else will work. Okay now for this stack to make sense The key concept behind this arm and taught me said he said that most speakers get on stage right and I okay This is what my package is boom number, you know first thing you get is this and then you get this and you get this And you get this and they show you all these cool things and then the last thing they show you is usually like just one of their bonuses Right, you're gonna get this really cool calculator watch or whatever, right? And then you're like, okay, and all it cost is $10,000 And so the problem is that people's attention span is like Super short right like like Shorter than you will ever believe right people's attention fan is just like almost not there And so when you say the price is only $10,000 the only thing they they associate that price with is the last bonus She showed them so if you showed them like all this cool stuff like you're gonna a car and a house and an airplane and a You know, I'm gonna name Mars after you and you're gonna school calculator watch and the price is only $10,000 All they're like is like all they they remember is like oh, it's 10 grand for a calculator watch and that sucks That's a lot of money, right? You said that that's how people's brains work And so what you have to do is you have to restack what's in the offer over and over and over again And right before you rebuild the price you have to restack again so they associate the price with everything Okay, so I'll show you how that works in a minute But if you see from these little images here like here I'm saying component one is this then I talk about it And I say okay now component number two you get this plus this I talk about that And see so that means you're gonna get this plus this plus this and I talk about it I see I keep stacking I keep restacking it then I show them the price and I move on I'll talk about the stuff here in a minute Okay, so let me show that works so I come through this presentation and I talk about the first thing so this is my stack slide so say okay So I just talked about first thing I'm getting they're gonna one year enterprise account to click phones for free So the first thing is one year enterprise account click phone So here's the first of my stack right you can get this total value of $3,564 And next thing you're gonna get my transition to my next thing you get the master class which is this and this this is why it's amazing Here's testimonials of people who loved it and how much money they made blah blah blah blah blah blah Who works for then I stack it again So that means you're gonna get the one year enterprise account to click phones and the six week funnel hacks masterclass total value $6,561 okay, so I stack the two parts of the offer so far now I transition to the third thing What's third thing you get you need traffic? Or I think they changed the name of it recently, but it used to be bill me later and you say yes What happens the paypal goes and does an instant credit check if they if they approved boom It drops $1,000 in their account they buy it you get the money instantly It didn't cost them a penny and they got six months with no payments and no interest Okay, this is one of the most powerful slides in my whole presentation. Well, that actually finances whole thing for you You know, it's not for everyone. You've got to be subject to your credit approval And you got to be in the u.s. But if if you qualify for this you sign that means you go in there You don't pay a penny right now. So then we made a clock that went 15 minutes And cells came in and at 15 minutes. They just the second the clock is zero people stopped buying I was like, well screw that. Let's put this this makes us a 30 minute clock So we bumped out 30 minutes and then I sit on for 30 minutes doing q&a And the whole 30 minutes people are buying and usually about 30 minutes is when it starts trickling down. It stops But it's crazy. I did webinars for almost a decade where I would do the close say the price and I'd end Where I know that more than half my I say it's probably three fourth of my cells come during this 28 minute Or just during this 30 minute countdown clock. Okay Like it it has made me so much more money. I don't know how I didn't do it before but it's amazing, right? People are like, how do you make a countdown clock in powerpoint? I just found a clock there on a clock online. I recorded in camtasia turned into a video file and I added the video to my slide It's just a video file so you can pause it And push play like it's not a real countdown clock. It's a little video because I couldn't figure out how to make a countdown clock So there you go. That's what I did Um, so count on clocks huge then the next question like what do you do for 30 minutes while the countdown clock's happening? Okay, same thing you do in the introduction. I just did to you guys right there. I just dropped three trial closes on you So some of you guys are saying what is a trial close? Trial close is something that I am embedding all throughout my presentations I learned this from ted thomas Ted thomas's nickname is the pied piper I've seen videos of him speaking at robert kisaki events where he will have a line of a thousand people All holding the credit cards above their head waving it Falling him to the back of the room and they call the pied piper because he's the best at like getting people to do that And so I was speaking at an event and uh, I had heard rumors of ted thomas never met him I'm doing my presentation and look out over the eyes and the back through my see this old man And I was like, I think that's ted thomas. I am so nervous right now that the king of closing is sitting here watching me speak Right, so I did my presentation and we did some cells and it wasn't like amazing But it was good and I was like kind of proud of it and he was kind of hanging out after the the webinar Excuse me the the seminar and then after everyone kind of disappeared he came to me said hey rustle My name is ted thomas. I'm like, oh, yeah, I know who you are. Don't worry and he's like can I take you to lunch? Like that would be awesome So he took me to lunch we start talking he starts asking all these questions question after question after question And then he stops and he says rustle look at your head right now And I looked at my head's like just nodding. He's like see your head's moving like yeah He's like what I just did you I just did a whole bunch of trial closes Which is why you are nodding. Yes right now He's like when I was in your room said you were a gauging speaker You're getting people to buy you know people went and bought things were good But I was sitting in the back of the room and I noticed that not a single person's head was moving in the entire room He said if you watch me speak from the second I stand on stage the entire presentation Everybody's heads nodding. Yes the whole time I'm like really it's like yeah, they've said yes to me about a million times before ever asking for money I was like, well, how do you do that? He said it's this little thing I call trial closes He said what a trial closes is you ask people Little questions a little yes or no questions and the answer is obviously yes And they say yes and they say yes again And even if you're home in a webinar your head is probably nodding when I'm doing my webinar, okay So I'm using these trial closes throughout the entire thing. So why why and they're not a success in the past one of them is like they You know, maybe they heard they had to get rid of carbs and so they love donuts They're not giving up donuts or bread or whatever, right? So it's like if you know that that's the majority people's Concerned you say look the cool thing is you can do this without cutting out carbs Like are you serious like I not cut out carbs like how is this possible, right? Or um, you know, they they've tried in the past and they lost them. They gained it right back. Okay. That's a common concern So, okay, how do we make this was not a this was a lifestyle change not a yoyo diet or whatever those things are so I think it really you know the key is is understanding your market And understanding like what pain they've gone through to get to where they're at right now, right people People take action they get on a webinar that they're buying things because they're they're um, they're usually in some kind of pain And they're trying to get out of that pain And so if you can identify like the last 10 times they tried to get out of that pain like what was the issues they probably ran into Like I know with my market, you know, the issues were They don't know what to do Okay, which is why we show them how to funnel. How now you know what to do I don't know how to do it. I can't afford programmers. So, okay, well, that's cool Let me show you how to do with click funnels and like, okay. Well, that's cool. They understand it. So here's conceptually now what we do We call this the perfect webinar funnel Okay People always ask me what day is the best day of your webinar and how long I should I start promoting and what should I All these things like that. So I'll kind of walk you through what we what we do and this isn't This isn't 100% like the same for every everything right like if this You know this a lot of these things are happening if if I have somebody who's sending emails from the webinar If I'm doing this on facebook, it's going to change a little bit, but not dramatically So just kind of keep that in mind that like this this process will tweak based on the type of traffic you're sending to it Okay, but I'm gonna I'm gonna kind of explain it Um as if I have a joint venture partner who's promoting this webinar And then after I'll kind of tweak and show you how we do if it's facebook or some other type of ad source Okay, so again, I call this the perfect webinar funnel. So in my perfect world. I like do my webinars on wednesday And then again on friday. Okay, so I'm doing two webinars each week. Okay, and uh the the reason is um a lot of people I know is funny A bunch of students who are doing webinars once they're doing like every thursday or whatever And so the webinar ended on thursday and friday started buying ads again They drive ads friday saturday sunday monday tuesday wednesday thursday and the show breaks were horrible And the first they're all freaking out. It's like, oh, nobody's showing up But we started digging deeper in the analytics what we found that was interesting is that Their show up rates people that registered friday saturday sunday monday tuesday were really low But people registered wednesday and thursday were awesome like they were all showing up We just because like most of people again, their attention spans are so short that they don't remember that far out And so we had to compress that time. So for me, I like about a two and a half day Lead time to a webinar if I go pat it maybe three and a half days max But not any longer than that. So that's why like wednesday because I like to start my promotion on monday Doing monday promotion tuesday wednesday wednesday then you do wednesday webinar And then kind of through there. Okay, because I don't I don't want more in two and a half days From when the first could hear about it to when the webinar happens if it goes further than that the show up rate Just starts taken. Okay. All right. So this idea. So monday, I would have a joint venture partner promote my webinar registration page Okay, and then percentage people will hit the confirmation page and then they're going to hopefully buy something right So you guys saw that process already Then after they register for the webinar, I send them what we call an indoctrination Process or some indoctrination pages. These are pages to get them excited about About the webinar. Okay. Now in a perfect world, what would happen is someone register on monday Then monday they watch video number one tuesday to watch video number two wednesday morning They watch video number three and then they show up on the webinar. Okay, and so many people stress that about like rustle I they register on on a tuesday and then the webinar happened and they got the other videos later and it's all messed up and I keep trying to stress like it doesn't really matter. Like these indoctrination pages are amplifiers. They're not necessary to the sale They're there to help uh strengthen the sale So a lot of times people register they'll watch video number one Then they get on the webinar then they watch video two and then video three and then they buy after video three Right this week. It doesn't matter kind of where where these hit It's just having them there because it intensifies Their desire and their likelihood to purchase when they're actually on the webinar. Okay, does that make sense? So it just don't stress out about like the timing the sequencing making sure it's perfect because it won't ever be So don't worry about it. Okay, so the register on monday Boom and then they get start getting each day they get video number one video number two video number three And somewhere in that time they're watching the webinar and i'm trying to sell them Okay, so we push really hard for this live wednesday webinar So monday mail tuesday mail wednesday we mail in the morning and then wednesday we mail an hour before the webinar actually happens again Okay, a couple other tricks I know joel knows these a couple other things we do if you're using go to webinar A cool trick you can do is go to webinar will send a message out an hour before the webinar saying don't forget your webinar starts in an hour So what we do is that we um 10 10 to 15 minutes before the webinar starts I log in to go to webinar and then I change the title of the webinar from You know russia's cool webinar to Astric astric starting now all in bold caps astric astric and then russia's cool webinar So I change the title of it and it says hey do you want to notify all participants of the change of title?
